role.title: Product Manager – Developer Platform
role.description: Nexus is hiring a Product Manager to own the roadmap for our developer platform, used by 20,000+ teams worldwide. You will collaborate with engineering, design, and GTM to ship features that delight developers.
role.role_summary: Drive the vision and roadmap for the Nexus developer platform, balancing short-term wins with long-term architectural bets.
responsibilities: Define and own the 12-month roadmap for the Nexus developer platform
Conduct continuous discovery with customers (interviews, surveys, data analysis)
Write clear, concise PRDs and acceptance criteria in partnership with engineering
Define success metrics and run A/B tests for major releases
Present roadmap to executive team quarterly
role.work_hours: Full-time, flexible — core overlap hours 13:00–17:00 UTC

preferred_qualifications: Prior experience as a software engineer or developer advocate
Familiarity with Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, or infrastructure tooling

benefits.description: €150/month health & wellbeing allowance (Gympass or insurance top-up)
€2,000/year L&D budget + access to all major product conferences
25 days PTO + local public holidays, async-first culture
16 weeks fully paid parental leave (primary and secondary)
€2,000 home office setup allowance on day one

growth.career_path: PM → Senior PM → Principal PM or Group PM
growth.mentorship: Weekly 1:1 with Head of Product + PM guild community of practice

team.name: Developer Platform
team.methodology: Dual-track agile with weekly discovery and delivery syncs
team.description: Cross-functional squad of 12 (4 engineers, 2 designers, 1 data analyst, 1 PM). We own the developer-facing API, CLI, SDKs, and docs portal.

process.decision_timeline: Feedback within 5 business days of each stage

culture.diversity_statement: We actively seek candidates from underrepresented backgrounds in tech.
culture.employer_overview: Nexus provides the deployment and observability backbone for 20,000+ engineering teams. Series B ($42M), 120 employees, fully remote.
